name: xschem-viewer-configurable
description: View XSchem .sch/.sym schematics in VS Code with configurable library search paths. Bundles the SKY130 and IHP SG13G2 device symbols so PDK schematics render with zero config.

scripts.test: node test/resolver.test.cjs && node test/config.test.cjs && node test/manifest.test.cjs

xschem.libraryPaths.markdownDescription:
Additional XSchem library search roots (behaves like `XSCHEM_LIBRARY_PATH`). Each entry is a **directory**; a symbol reference such as `sg13g2_pr/sg13_lv_nmos.sym` is looked up as `<root>/sg13g2_pr/sg13_lv_nmos.sym`. Roots are searched in order, after the extension's bundled libraries and before the schematic-relative fallback. Entries that don't resolve to an existing directory are skipped (enable `#xschem.resolveDebug#` to log skips).

Variables: `${env:VAR}`; a leading `~`; `${workspaceFolder:NAME}` (a **named** folder from a multi-root workspace — the unambiguous form); and bare `${workspaceFolder}` (the opened schematic's own workspace folder — in a multi-root workspace this is the innermost/most-specific folder, e.g. a submodule root, **not** the meta-root). Absolute paths are used verbatim; relative paths resolve against the schematic's workspace folder.

Example (this workspace):
```json
"xschem.libraryPaths": [
  "${env:PDK_ROOT}/ihp-sg13g2/libs.tech/xschem",
  "${workspaceFolder:platform}/docker/xschem_library"
]
```
Or with absolute paths (never ambiguous):
```json
"xschem.libraryPaths": [
  "/home/you/pdks/ihp-sg13g2/libs.tech/xschem",
  "/abs/path/to/spicexplorer-platform/docker/xschem_library"
]
```
(In-repo `shared/…` and sibling-block symbols are picked up automatically by `#xschem.autoDetectXschemrc#`.)

xschem.autoDetectXschemrc.markdownDescription: Walk up from the opened schematic's folder to the workspace root and add every directory that contains an `xschemrc` file as a library search root, **and** parse that file's `append XSCHEM_LIBRARY_PATH` lines (resolving the `[file dirname [info script]]` idiom). So in-repo `shared/…` and sibling-block symbols resolve with no manual configuration. Parsed roots are only added if they exist and lie inside the workspace; lines using `$env(...)` or `source` are skipped — PDK symbols outside the repo stay opt-in via an explicit `#xschem.libraryPaths#` entry.

xschem.includeWorkspaceFolders.markdownDescription: When enabled, allow the viewer to load symbol/schematic files from the schematic's **own** workspace folder (enabling relative `../` sub-block references anywhere within that folder). Off by default to keep the webview's file-read scope minimal — the schematic's directory plus the configured `#xschem.libraryPaths#` (and auto-detected `xschemrc` roots) are always available regardless of this setting. Only the schematic's own workspace folder is exposed, never sibling roots of a multi-root workspace.

xschem.resolveDebug.description: Log every symbol-resolution attempt to the webview Developer Tools console (Help > Toggle Developer Tools). Use this to debug "symbol not found" problems and see exactly which paths were tried.

author: NooriDan
